patches.find: The dome presents a "live monitoring dashboard" updating every five minutes, but direct examination of monitor.py (commit 8e4bf9b) reveals that {{CA_HARDCODED}} of {{CA_REVIEWED}} monitored domains compare static predicted values against static observed values — checks that cannot fail by construction. Only {{CA_LIVE}} WINs fetch live external data — all from globe-calibrated sources — and the NMP Drift Rate domain uses an adaptive tolerance with a 50% error floor.
patches.replace: Direct examination of monitor.py (commit 8e4bf9b) reveals that {{CA_NONE}} of {{CA_REVIEWED}} reviewed WINs have no validation code at all — over half the dome's "confirmed predictions" are completely unmonitored. Of the remainder, {{CA_HARDCODED}} use hardcoded validation where pred==obs, and only {{CA_LIVE}} fetch live data — all from globe-calibrated sources.
patches.rationale: ISS-913: TLDR must lead with none/unmonitored finding (the primary shock from EXP-089 restructure), not with hardcoded monitoring. The word 'unmonitored' never appeared in the TLDR.

patches.find: Eight are self-contradicted by the dome's own geometry (WIN-044/050/051 tidal, WIN-053 flux, WIN-056 solar, WIN-061 P-wave, WIN-067 gravity, WIN-068 tidal model) — monitoring would expose the contradiction. Fifteen are misleading (duplicated, circular, or non-discriminating). Eleven are standard-physics relabellings — monitoring would confirm the phenomenon but not the dome's causal explanation.
patches.replace: Seven are self-contradicted by the dome's own geometry (WIN-044/050/051 tidal, WIN-053 flux, WIN-056 solar, WIN-061 P-wave, WIN-067 gravity) — monitoring would expose the contradiction. Fifteen are misleading (duplicated, circular, or non-discriminating). Twelve are standard-physics relabellings — monitoring would confirm the phenomenon but not the dome's causal explanation.
patches.rationale: ISS-914: WIN-068 verdict=Std Model Explains, not Self-Contradicted. Remove from SC list, correct count Eight→Seven. ISS-917: knock-on fix Eleven→Twelve for std-physics count.

patches.find: Of the dome's 16 self-contradicted WINs (Part 2), eight have no monitoring at all — but the other eight (WIN-002/010/012/029/038/045/046/049) do have monitoring domains, all hardcoded to pass. The dome does not merely neglect to test its geometric contradictions; for half of them, it builds validation infrastructure that cannot fail by construction.
patches.replace: Of the dome's 14 self-contradicted WINs (Part 2), seven have no monitoring at all — but the other seven (WIN-002/012/029/038/045/046/049) do have monitoring domains, all hardcoded to pass. The dome does not merely neglect to test its geometric contradictions; for half of them, it builds validation infrastructure that cannot fail by construction.
patches.rationale: ISS-915: Total SC WINs is 14, not 16. ISS-916: WIN-010 verdict=Std Model Explains — remove from list. Both halves become 7 not 8.
ISS-918: Duplicate style attribute on h2 element. Remove the redundant copy.

patches.find: The dome model presents its own evidence hierarchy — a Steelman Evidence Ladder ranking its strongest claims. This review applies a complementary structure, organized by the type of argument rather than by topic, because argument type determines how hard a conclusion is to escape:
patches.replace: This review applies a three-tier structure organized by the type of argument rather than by topic, because argument type determines how hard a conclusion is to escape. The dome model's own stated priority is prospective prediction ('The strongest claim this registry makes is prospective prediction' — wins page). Our tier system is organized by a different axis: how hard each argument type is to escape, from internally self-contradictory through externally falsified:
patches.rationale: ISS-919 CRITICAL: 'Steelman Evidence Ladder' was falsely attributed to the dome model. This term is OUR analytical construct from Part 6. The dome has no 'evidence ladder' — only a stated priority for prospective prediction. Rewrite removes the false attribution and correctly describes the dome's actual stated priority.

patches.find: When a model's geometry predicts a Schumann frequency of 22–35 Hz but the observed value is 7.83 Hz
patches.replace: When a model's geometry predicts a Schumann frequency of ~22 Hz but the observed value is 7.83 Hz
patches.rationale: ISS-921: Roadmap uses '22–35 Hz' but Section 2.1 heading uses '~22 Hz'. Use ~22 Hz to match the section readers click through to.

patches.find: A reader who trusts only the dome's own numbers and reads only Parts 2 and 2b will find the model already refutes itself. The remaining parts confirm and extend that conclusion with independent evidence from progressively broader domains.
patches.replace: A reader who reads only Parts 2 and 2b will find the model already refutes itself — Part 2 from the dome's own geometry, Part 2b from the dome's own automation code. The remaining parts confirm and extend that conclusion with independent evidence from progressively broader domains.
patches.rationale: ISS-922: 'trusts only the dome's own numbers' is overstated for Part 2b (code analysis uses our own audit methodology). Fix to accurately describe what each part contributes.

patches.find: The V13 Finsler formula was calibrated against the known Indian Pacific railway distance — that is curve-fitting, not prediction. It matches a circuitous rail route, not the geometric city-pair distance, and fails by 78% when tested on Sydney–Buenos Aires. Two internal methods (Finsler vs MDS scaffold) disagree by 460 km on Sydney–Perth itself.
patches.replace: The V13 Finsler formula was calibrated against the known Indian Pacific railway distance — that is curve-fitting, not prediction. Its predecessor (V12) failed by 78% on Sydney–Buenos Aires; V13 claims −8.4% via unpublished scaling parameters that cannot be independently verified. Two internal methods (Finsler vs MDS scaffold) disagree by 460 km on Sydney–Perth itself.
patches.rationale: ISS-927: TLDR says V13 fails by 78% but body correctly says that's V12's error (V13 claims -8.4%). TLDR-body contradiction resolved by clearly distinguishing V12 error from V13 claim.

patches.find: • One test is failing by nearly 40% — passing only via a widened tolerance and a three-year consecutive requirement that allows single-year escapes.
patches.replace: • One test shows 39.9% error — counted as PASS only via a widened tolerance and a three-year consecutive requirement that allows single-year escapes.
patches.rationale: ISS-929: Closing says 'failing by nearly 40%' but opening (correctly) says dome self-reports as PASS. Avoid the contradiction: report the error percentage while explaining why the dome counts it as passing.

patches.find: within measurement noise (Polaris: claimed excess smaller than the star's own polar offset)
patches.replace: within measurement noise (Polaris: claimed excess smaller than the star's own polar offset, and the dome's broader Polaris-latitude prediction is self-marked FALSIFIED per the dome's own FAIL-001)
patches.rationale: ISS-930: The dome's own site marks the broader Polaris-latitude prediction as FALSIFIED (FAIL-001). This is a stronger argument than just noting measurement noise — it's the dome's own admission.
